title: More Love to Thee O Christ
author: Elizabeth Prentiss
story: Prentiss wrote this during a period of deep grief after losing two children. She kept it hidden for years before her husband discovered and published it. Its honest prayer for deeper love through suffering resonates with all who grieve.

lines: More love to Thee, O Christ
Hear Thou the pray'r I make On bended knee
This is my earnest plea, More love, O Christ, to Thee, More love to Thee
Once earthly joy I craved, Sought peace and rest
Now thee a lone I seek, Give what is best
This all my pray'r shall be, More love, O Christ, to Thee, More love to Thee
Then shall my latest breath, Whisper Thy praise, This be the parting cry My heart shall raise
This still its pray'r shall be, More love, O Christ, to Thee, More love to Thee
